---
format:
  amaquarto-docx: default
  amaquarto-pdf:
    pdf-format: manuscript
    keep-tex: false
    number-sections: false
bibliography: bibliography.bib
# NOTE: The Web Appendix is uploaded as a SEPARATE PDF file.
# Prior to acceptance, omit author names for anonymity.
# Upon acceptance, add author names and contact details below the title.
# Do NOT include 'title:' or 'abstract:' in this YAML header;
# the cover page content is placed directly in the body below.
---

```{=latex}
% Web appendix includes page numbers starting at 1 (AMA requirement).
% Manuscript mode suppresses page numbers, so we restore them here.
\fancypagestyle{plain}{%
  \fancyhf{}%
  \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}%
  \fancyfoot[C]{\small\thepage}%
}
\pagestyle{plain}
\setcounter{page}{1}
```

::: {.center}
**Web Appendix**

**Your Manuscript Title Here**
:::

<!-- Upon acceptance, uncomment and complete the following:

::: {.center}
First Author Full Name, Title, Institution, email@example.com

Second Author Full Name, Title, Institution, email@example.com
:::
-->

\ 

::: {.noindent}
Web Appendix A: Descriptive Title

Web Appendix B: Descriptive Title

Web Appendix C: Descriptive Title

<!-- NOTE: The above is a manual table of contents.
Update titles and add/remove entries to match your actual web appendix
sections. The AMA requires a table of contents with page numbers on
page 1 if the web appendix has multiple sections. Page numbers can be
added after the final PDF is compiled. -->

\ 

These materials have been supplied by the authors to aid in the understanding of their paper. The AMA is sharing these materials at the request of the authors.
:::

{{< pagebreak >}}

<!--
  WEB APPENDIX GUIDELINES:
  =========================================
  - Use Times New Roman 12-point font.
  - Use the same heading styles as in the main paper.
  - If multiple sections: label clearly as "Web Appendix A," "Web Appendix B."
  - Each section begins on a new page.
  - Include page numbers, beginning with 1.
  - Tables: use "W" prefix (Table W1, Table W2).
  - Figures: use "W" prefix (Figure W1, Figure W2).
  - Equations: number as W1, W2, ..., WN.
  - References cited only here should be listed at the end of this file.
  - The web appendix does NOT count toward the 50-page limit.
  - Upon acceptance, submit as a single PDF.
-->

# Web Appendix A: Descriptive Title {.unnumbered}

This web appendix provides detailed information about the methodology used to
conduct the studies presented in the main article. Replace this text with your
content.

## Heading {.unnumbered}

Replace this with your content. Tables in the web appendix use the "W" prefix:

| | **Column 1** | **Column 2** | **Column 3** |
|---|---|---|---|
| Variable 1 | | | |
| Variable 2 | | | |
| Variable 3 | | | |

: Table W1: Descriptive Table Title. {#tbl-w1}

*Notes*: Table W1 general notes. Report actual *p*-values instead of adding
asterisks to represent thresholds.

{{< pagebreak >}}

# Web Appendix B: Descriptive Title {.unnumbered}

Replace this with your content. Equations in the web appendix are numbered with
a "W" prefix.

$$
Y_i = \alpha + \gamma Z_i + \nu_i
$$ {#eq-w1}

{{< pagebreak >}}

# Web Appendix C: Descriptive Title {.unnumbered}

Replace this with your content. Figures in the web appendix use the "W" prefix
(e.g., Figure W1, Figure W2). Include error bars in bar graphs where applicable,
and specify in the figure notes what they represent (e.g., $\pm$1 SE).

{{< pagebreak >}}

# References {.unnumbered}

<!-- Only include references that are cited exclusively in the Web Appendix. -->

::: {#refs}
:::
